brawler.name: Brawler
description: ## Brawler
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/23›

Deadly even with nothing in her hands, a brawler eschews using the fighter's heavy armor and the monk's mysticism, focusing instead on perfecting many styles of brutal unarmed combat. Versatile, agile, and able to adapt to most enemy attacks, a brawler's body is a powerful weapon.
**Role:** Brawlers are maneuverable and well suited for creating flanking situations or dealing with lightly armored enemies, as well as quickly adapting to a rapidly changing battlefield.
::class{any hd=10 parent="‹class/Fighter› and ‹class/monk›." wealth=3 skp=4 acro climb craft ea ha intm kd kl per prof ride sm swim}

::prof{simple weaps="handaxe~short sword" wExtra="weapons from the close ‹ability/fighter »weapon group<s›" light shields}
::ab[Brawler's Cunning (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="If your Intelligence score is less than 13, it counts as 13 for the purpose of meeting the prerequisites of ‹main/combat feat«s›."}

::ab[Martial Flexibility (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power move="You gain the benefit of a combat feat you don't possess. This effect lasts for 1 minute. You must meet all the feat's prerequisites." special="You can use this ability again before the duration expires in order to replace the previous combat feat with another choice. If a combat feat has a daily use limitation (such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist›), any uses of that combat feat while using this ability count toward that feat's daily limit." useInc3="2~brawler" imp6="You can use this ability to gain the benefit of two combat feats at the same time. You may select one feat as a ***swift action*** or two feats as a **move** action. You may use one of these feats to meet a prerequisite of the second feat; doing so means that you cannot replace a feat currently fulfilling another's prerequisite without also replacing those feats that require it. Each individual feat selected counts toward your daily uses of this ability." hImp imp10="You can use this ability to gain the benefit of three combat feats at the same time. You may select one feat as a ***free*** action, two feats as a ***swift*** action, or three feats as a ***move*** action. You may use one of the feats to meet a prerequisite of the second and third feats, and use the second feat to meet a prerequisite of the third feat. Each individual feat selected counts toward your daily uses of this ability." imp12="You gain the benefit of one combat feat as an ***immediate action*** or three combat feats as a ***swift*** action. Each individual feat selected counts toward your daily uses of this ability." imp20="You can use this ability to gain the benefit of any number of combat feats as a ***swift*** action. Each feat selected counts toward your daily uses of this ability."}

::ab[Martial Training (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="You count your total brawler levels as both fighter levels and monk levels for the purpose of qualifying for feats. You also count as both a fighter and a monk for feats and magic items that have different effects based on whether the character has levels in those classes (such as *Stunning Fist* and a ‹magic-wondrous/monk's robe›)." special="This ability does not automatically grant feats normally granted to fighters and monks based on class level, namely *Stunning Fist.*"}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{jl=strip l=1 icon=melee ability="You gain ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. You may attack with fists, elbows, knees, and feet. This means that you may make unarmed strikes with your hands full. You apply your full Strength modifier (not half) on damage rolls for all your unarmed strikes. Your unarmed strikes deal lethal damage, although you can choose to deal nonlethal damage with no penalty on the attack roll. You can make this choice while grappling as well." special="Your unarmed strike is treated as both a manufactured weapon and a natural weapon for the purpose of spells and effects that enhance or improve either manufactured weapons or natural weapons. The damage of your strike is determined by your size and level, shown on the chart below."}
:::div{className=firstColumnEnd}
| Level | Damage (Small Brawler) | Damage (Medium Brawler) | Damage (Large Brawler) |

::ab[Bonus Combat Feats]{jl=strip l=2 icon=power ability="You gain a bonus combat feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. This feat must be one that affect or improve your defenses or melee attacks. You must meet the prerequisites of the selected bonus combat feat." imp5="You gain a new bonus combat feat, using the same limitations as above. You can choose to learn a new bonus combat feat in place of a bonus combat feat you have already learned. In effect, you lose the bonus combat feat in exchange for the new one. The old feat cannot be one that was used as a prerequisite for another feat, prestige class, or other ability. You can only change one feat, and must choose whether or not to swap the feat at the time you gain the bonus combat feat." imp8="You gain a new bonus combat feat and may swap out a previous combat feat (with the same limitations as above)." imp11="You gain a new bonus combat feat and may swap out a previous combat feat (with the same limitations as above)." imp14="You gain a new bonus combat feat and may swap out a previous combat feat (with the same limitations as above)." imp17="You gain a new bonus combat feat and may swap out a previous combat feat (with the same limitations as above)." imp20="You gain a new bonus combat feat and may swap out a previous combat feat (with the same limitations as above)."}

::ab[Brawler's Flurry (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=melee fullround="You can make a *brawler's flurry* as a full-attack action. When doing so, you have the ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting› feat when attacking with any combination of unarmed strikes, weapons from the close fighter weapon group, or weapons with the &quot&monk&quot& special feature. You do not need to use two different weapons to use this ability." special="You apply your full Strength modifier to your damage rolls for all attacks made with *brawler's flurry,* whether the attacks are made with an off-hand weapon or a weapon wielded in both hands. You can substitute ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, and ‹rule/trip› combat maneuvers for unarmed attacks as part of *brawler's flurry.* You can't use natural weapons as part of *brawler's flurry,* nor can you make natural weapon attacks in addition to your *brawler's flurry* attacks." imp8="You gain use of the ‹feat/Improved Two-Weapon Fighting› feat when using *brawler's flurry.*" imp15="You gain use of the ‹feat/Greater Two-Weapon Fighting› feat when using *brawler's flurry.*"}

::ab[Maneuver Training (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=melee-def passive="You can select one ‹rule/combat maneuver› to receive additional training. You gain a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks when performing that combat maneuver and a +1 bonus to your CMD when defending against that maneuver." imp7="You select a new combat maneuver and gain the +1 bonuses above, while the previous maneuver's bonuses become +2." imp11="You select a new combat maneuver and gain the +1 bonuses above, while the previous maneuvers' bonuses become +2 and +3." imp15="You select a new combat maneuver and gain the +1 bonuses above, while the previous maneuvers' bonuses become +2, +3, and +4." imp19="You select a new combat maneuver and gain the +1 bonuses above, while the previous maneuvers' bonuses become +2, +3, +4, and +5."}

::ab[AC Bonus (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=def passive="When you wear light or no armor, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC and CMD. These bonuses to AC apply against touch attacks." special="You lose these bonuses while immobilized or ‹misc/helpless›, wearing medium or heavy armor, or carrying a medium or heavy load." imp9="This bonus becomes +2." imp13="This bonus becomes +3." imp18="This bonus becomes +4."}

::ab[Knockout (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=melee l=4 usage="Once a day." standard="You can unleash a devastating attack that can instantly knock a target unconscious. You must announce this intent before making your attack roll. If you hit and the target takes damage from the blow, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 your brawler level + the higher of your Strength or Dexterity modifier) or fall unconscious for 1d6 rounds." hSpecial special="Each round on its turn, the unconscious target may attempt a new saving throw to end the effect as a ***full-round action*** that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Creatures immune to critical hits or nonlethal damage are immune to this ability." imp10="You can use this twice a day." imp16="You can use this three times per day."}

::ab[Brawler's Strike (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=melee-boost passive="Your unarmed strikes are treated as magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp9="Your unarmed attacks are also treated as cold iron and silver for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp12="You choose one alignment component: chaotic, evil, good, or lawful; your unarmed strikes also count as this alignment for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. (This alignment component cannot be the opposite of your actual alignment, such as a good brawler choosing evil strikes.)" imp17="Your unarmed attacks are also treated as ‹eq-material/adamantine› weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction and bypassing hardness."}

::ab[Close Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=melee passive="Your damage with weapons from the &quot&close&quot& weapon group increases. When wielding a *close weapon,* you use the unarmed strike damage of you 4 levels lower instead of the base damage for that weapon (for example, a 5th-level Medium brawler wielding a ‹eq-weapon/punching dagger› deals 1d6 points of damage instead of the weapon's normal 1d4). If the weapon normally deals more damage than this, its damage is unchanged." special="This ability does not affect any other aspect of the weapon. You can decide to use the weapon's base damage instead of your adjusted unarmed strike damage - this must be declared before the attack roll is made."}

::ab[Awesome Blow (Ex)]{jl=strip l=16 icon=melee standard="You can perform an *awesome blow* combat maneuver against a corporeal creature of your size or smaller. If the combat maneuver check succeeds, the opponent takes damage as if you hit it with the *close weapon* you are wielding or an unarmed strike, it is knocked flying 10 feet in a direction of your choice, and it falls ‹rule/prone›." special="You can only push the opponent in a straight line, and the opponent can't move closer to you than the square it started in. If an obstacle prevents the completion of the opponent's move, the opponent and the obstacle each take 1d6 points of damage, and the opponent is knocked prone in the space adjacent to the obstacle. (Unlike the ‹feat/Awesome Blow› monster feat, you can be of any size to use this ability.)"}

::ab[Improved Awesome Blow (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="You can use your *awesome blow* ability as an attack rather than as a standard action. You may use it on creatures of any size. If the maneuver roll is a natural 20, you can immediately attempt to confirm the critical by rolling another combat maneuver check with all the same modifiers as the one just rolled; if the confirmation roll is successful, the attack deals double damage, and the damage from hitting an obstacle (if any) is also doubled."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Perfect Warrior (Ex)]{oldDogNewTricks capstone="At 20th level, the brawler has reached the highest levels of her art. The brawler's maneuver training increases by 2 and her dodge bonus to AC improves by 2. This replaces the 20th-level improvement to martial flexibility."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Dwarf:** Reduce the hardness of any object made from clay, stone, or metal by 1 whenever the object is struck by the brawler's unarmed strike (minimum 0).[^A]
**Elf:** Add 1 foot to the brawler's base speed. In combat, this has an effect only for every five increases in the brawler's base speed.[^A]
**Gnome:** Increase the number of times per day the brawler can use martial flexibility by 1/4.[^B]
**Half-elf:** Add 1 to the brawler's CMD when she's resisting a trip or overrun combat maneuver.[^B]
**Halfling:** Gain a +1 bonus to the brawler's CMD when resisting a grapple or overrun combat maneuver.[^B]
**Half-orc:** Add 1/4 to the brawler's effective level to determine her unarmed strike damage.[^C]
**Human:** Gain a +1 bonus to the brawler's CMD when resisting two combat maneuvers of the brawler's choice.[^C]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^D]
**Kitsune:** Increase the number of times per day the brawler can use martial flexibility by 1/4.[^D]
**Strix:** Add 1/4 to the brawler's effective class level to determine her unarmed strike damage.[^E]
**Tengu:** Gain a +1/2 insight bonus on attack rolls made with the beak as a secondary natural attack (to a maximum of +3); the brawler must have a beak natural attack to select this option.[^F]
**Vanara:** Gain 1/5 of a new Fleet feat.[^G]

summoner.name: Summoner
description: ## Summoner
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/54›

While many who dabble in the arcane become adept at beckoning monsters from the farthest reaches of the planes, none are more skilled at it than the summoner. This practitioner of the arcane arts forms a close bond with one particular outsider, known as an eidolon, who gains power as the summoner becomes more proficient at his summoning. Over time, the two become linked, eventually even sharing a shard of the same soul. But this power comes with a price: the summoner's spells and abilities are limited due to his time spent enhancing the power and exploring the nature of his eidolon.

**Role:** Summoners spend much of their time exploring the arcane arts alongside their eidolons. While their power comes from within, they rely heavily on their eidolon companions in dangerous situations. While a summoner and his eidolon function as individuals, their true power lies in what they can accomplish together.
::class{any hd=8 wealth=2 skp=2 craft fly ha ka kd ke kg kh kl kna kno kp kr ling prof ride spc umd}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| 1st | +0 | +0 | +0 | +2 | Cantrips, eidolon, life link, *summon monster I* | 1 | - | - | - | - | - |
### Spells Known
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |

::prof{simple light extra="A summoner can cast summoner spells while wearing light armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance. Like any other arcane spellcaster, a summoner wearing medium or heavy armor, or using a shield, incurs a chance of arcane spell failure if the spell in question has a somatic component. A multiclass summoner still incurs the normal arcane spell failure chance for arcane spells received from other classes."}
::spellAb{caster=summoner type=arcane stat=Charisma spontaneous m limited trade53 tradeLimit}
::cantrips

::ab[Eidolon (Basics)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power ability="You begin play with the ability to summon to your side a powerful outsider called an eidolon. Your ‹sidekick/eidolon› forms a link with you, who, forever after, summons an aspect of the same creature. Your eidolon has the same alignment as you and can speak all of your languages. Eidolons are treated as ‹spelldef/summon«ed creatures<ing›, except that they are not sent back to their home plane until reduced to a number of negative hit points equal to or greater than their Constitution score. In addition, due to its tie to its summoner, an eidolon can touch and attack creatures warded by ‹spell/protection from evil› and similar effects that prevent contact with summoned creatures."}

A summoner can summon his eidolon in a ritual that takes 1 minute to perform. When summoned in this way, the eidolon hit points are unchanged from the last time it was summoned. The only exception to this is if the eidolon was slain, in which case it returns with half its normal hit points. The eidolon does not heal naturally. The eidolon remains until dismissed by the summoner (a standard action). If the eidolon is sent back to its home plane due to death, it cannot be summoned again until the following day. The eidolon cannot be sent back to its home plane by means of ‹spell/dispel magic›, but spells such as ‹spell/dismissal› and ‹spell/banishment› work normally. If the summoner is unconscious, asleep, or killed, his eidolon is immediately banished.

The eidolon takes a form shaped by the summoner's desires. The eidolon's Hit Dice, saving throws, skills, feats, and abilities are tied to the summoner's class level and increase as the summoner gains levels. In addition, each eidolon receives a pool of evolution points, based on the summoner's class level, that can be used to give the eidolon different abilities and powers, called ‹ability/evolutions›. Whenever the summoner gains a level, he must decide how these points are spent, and they are set until he gains another level of summoner.

The eidolon's physical appearance is up to the summoner, but it always appears as some sort of fantastical creature. This control is not fine enough to make the eidolon appear like a specific creature. The eidolon also bears a glowing rune that is identical to a rune that appears on the summoner's forehead as long as the eidolon is summoned. While this rune can be hidden through mundane means, it cannot be concealed through magic that changes appearance, such as ‹spell/alter self› or ‹spell/polymorph› (although ‹spell/invisibility› does conceal it as long as the spell lasts).

::ab[Life Link (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=protect order=fp free="Whenever your eidolon takes enough damage to send it back to its home plane, you can sacrifice any number of hit points. Each hit point sacrificed in this way prevents 1 point of damage done to your eidolon. This can prevent your eidolon from being sent back to its home plane." passive="In addition, you and your eidolon must remain within 100 feet of one another for your eidolon to remain at full strength. If your eidolon is beyond 100 feet but closer than 1,000 feet, its current and maximum hit point totals are reduced by 50%. If your eidolon is more than 1,000 feet away but closer than 10,000 feet, its current and maximum hit point totals are reduced by 75%. If your eidolon is more than 10,000 feet away, it is immediately returned to its home plane. Current hit points lost in this way are not restored when your eidolon gets closer to its summoner, but its maximum hit point total does return to normal."}

::ab[Summon Monster I (Sp)]{jl="Summon Monster" l=1 icon=magic useMod3=Cha standard="You can cast ‹spell/summon monster I› as a spell-like ability. Drawing upon this ability uses up the same power as you use to call your eidolon. As a result, you can only use this ability when your eidolon is not summoned. When you cast this spell, the creatures remain for 1 minute per level (instead of 1 round per level)." special="You cannot have more than one *summon monster* or *gate* spell active in this way at one time. If this ability is used again, any existing *summon monster* or *gate* immediately ends. These summon spells are considered to be part of your spell list for the purposes of spell trigger and spell completion items. In addition, you can expend uses of this ability to fufill the construction requirements of any magic item you create, so long as you can use this ability to cast the required spell." imp3="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster II›." imp5="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster III›." imp7="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster IV›." imp9="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster V›." imp11="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VI›." imp13="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VII›." imp15="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VIII›." imp17="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster IX›." imp19="This now behaves as either *summon monster IX* or ‹spell/gate›. If used as *gate,* you must pay any required material components."}

::ab[Bond Senses (Su)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=boost useL=summoner standard="You can share the senses of your eidolon, hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, and touching everything your eidolon does. There is no range to this effect, but your eidolon and you must be on the same plane. You can end this effect as a ***free action.***" hStandard}

::ab[Shield Ally (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=def passive="Whenever you are within your eidolon's reach, you receive a +2 shield bonus to your Armor Class and a +2 circumstance bonus on your saving throws. This bonus does not apply if your eidolon is ‹rule/grappled›, ‹misc/helpless›, ‹rule/paralyzed›, ‹misc/stunned›, or ‹misc/unconscious›."}

::ab[Maker's Call (Su)]{jl=strip l=6 icon=magic standard="You can call your eidolon to your side. This functions as ‹spell/dimension door›, using your caster level. When used, your eidolon appears adjacent to you (or as close as possible if all adjacent spaces are occupied). If your eidolon is out of range, the ability is wasted." useInc="4~summoner~6"}

::ab[Transposition (Su)]{jl=strip l=8 icon=magic standard="You can use your *maker's call* ability to swap locations with your eidolon. If it is larger than you, you can appear in any square occupied by your eidolon. The eidolon must occupy the square that was occupied by you if able, or as close as possible if it is not able."}

::ab[Aspect (Su)]{jl=strip l=10 icon=boost ability="You can divert up to 2 points from your eidolon's evolution pool to add evolutions to yourself. You cannot select any evolution that your eidolon could not possess, and you must be able to meet the requirements as well. You cannot select the *ability increase* evolution through this ability. Any points spent in this way are taken from your eidolon's evolution pool (reducing the total number available to your eidolon). You can change the evolutions you receive from these points any time you can change your eidolon's evolutions."}

::ab[Greater Shield Ally (Su)]{jl=strip l=12 icon=protect passive="Whenever an ally is within your eidolon's reach, the ally receives a +2 shield bonus to its Armor Class and a +2 circumstance bonus on its saving throws. If this ally is you, these bonuses increase to +4. This bonus does not apply if your eidolon is grappled, helpless, paralyzed, stunned, or unconscious."}

::ab[Life Bond (Su)]{jl=strip l=14 icon=def passive="As long as your eidolon has 1 or more hit points, you are protected from harm. Damage in excess of that which would reduce you to fewer than 0 hit points is instead transferred to your eidolon. This damage is transferred 1 point at a time, meaning that as soon as your eidolon is reduced to a number of negative hit points equal to its Constitution score, all excess damage remains with you. Effects that cause death but not damage are unaffected by this ability. This ability does not affect spells like ‹spell/baleful polymorph›, ‹spell/flesh to stone›, or ‹spell/imprisonment›, or other spells that do not cause actual damage."}

::ab[Merge Forms (Su)]{jl=strip l=16 order=urw icon=def-power useL=summoner fullround="You can touch your eidolon and merge forms with it. This transformation includes all of your gear. While merged in this way, you are protected from harm and cannot be the target of spells or effects. All effects and spells currently targeting you are suspended until you emerge from your eidolon (although durations continue to expire)." special="You can cast spells while inside your eidolon by taking control of your eidolon for the duration of the casting. Any material components used for these spells are taken from your gear, even though they are otherwise inaccessible. You can direct all of your eidolon's actions while merged, can perceive through its senses, and can speak through its voice. If your eidolon is returned to its home plane while you are merged with it, you are immediately ejected, taking 4d6 points of damage, and are stunned for 1 round." swift="You can end this effect at any time. You emerge in a square adjacent to your eidolon if able."}

::ab[Greater Aspect (Su)]{jl=strip l=18 icon=boost ability="You can divert more of your eidolon's evolutions to yourself. This ability functions as the *aspect* ability, but up to 6 evolution points can be taken. Unlike the *aspect* ability, your eidolon loses 1 point from its evolution pool for every 2 points (or fraction thereof) diverted to you."}

::ab[Twin Eidolon (Su)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=power standard="You can assume the shape of your eidolon, copying all of its evolutions, form, and abilities. Your Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores change to match the base scores of your eidolon. You can choose to have any gear that you carry become absorbed by your new form, as with spells from the polymorph subschool. Items with continuous effects continue to function while absorbed in this way. You lose your natural attacks and all racial traits (except bonus feats, skills, and languages) in favor of the abilities granted by your eidolon's evolutions. You retain all of your class features. You can end this effect as a ***free action.***" hStandard useL=summoner useUnit=minute useNC="1-minute"}

::altCapstone[Grand Eidolon (Su)]{deepMagics capstone="At 20th level, the summoner's eidolon becomes an absolute monstrosity - a thing of legend and terror. The summoner gains 4 additional evolution points to spend as he likes, not subject to subtype-based requirements."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Add DR 1/evil to the summoner's eidolon. Each additional time the summoner selects this benefit, the DR/evil increases by +1/2 (maximum DR 10/evil).[^A]
**Aquatic elf:** As long as the summoner's eidolon has a swim speed, add 5 feet to its swim speed (max +20 feet).[^B]
**Drow:** Add 1 hit point or 1 skill rank to the summoner's eidolon.[^C]
**Dwarf:** Add a +1/4 natural armor bonus to the AC of the summoner's eidolon.[^D]
**Elf:** The amount of time the summoner must spend to summon his eidolon is reduced by 1 round, to a minimum of 1 round.[^E]

**Fetchling:** The summoner's eidolon gains resistance 1 against either cold or electricity. Each time the summoner selects this reward, he increases his eidolon's resistance to one of those energy types by 1 (maximum 10 for any one energy type).[^F]
**Gathlain:** Add 1/4 evolution point to the eidolon's evolution pool. These bonus evolution points must be spent on evolutions that grant spell-like abilities or protect the eidolon from spells.[^G]
**Gnome:** Add +1 hit point to the summoner's eidolon.[^H] [^I]

**Goblin:** Add +1/4 evolution point to the eidolon's evolution pool. These bonus evolution points must be spent on evolutions that deal fire damage or protect the eidolon from fire (for example, resistance, energy attacks, immunity, breath weapon, and so on).[^J]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/4 to the eidolon's evolution pool.[^K] [^L]
**Halfling:** Add +1 skill rank to the summoner's eidolon.[^M] [^N]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 hit point to the summoner's eidolon.[^O]
**Human:** Add +1 hit point or +1 skill rank to the summoner's eidolon.[^P] [^Q]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^R]
**Kobold:** Add +1/4 to the summoner's shield ally bonus (maximum +2).[^S]
**Nagaji:** Add +1 hit point to the summoner's eidolon.[^T]
**Oread:** Add a +1/4 natural armor bonus to the AC of the summoner's eidolon.[^U]
**Ratfolk:** Add a +1 bonus on saving throws against poison made by the summoner's eidolon.[^V]
**Tiefling:** Add +1 hit point or +1 skill rank to the summoner's eidolon.[^W]
**Undine:** If the summoner's eidolon has an aquatic base form, add +5 feet to the range of the summoner's life link ability.[^X]
**Vanara:** Add 1/4 to the eidolon's evolution pool.[^Y]
**Wayang:** Add +1 skill rank to the summoner's eidolon.[^Z]

::archetype[Blood Summoner]{c=summoner r="Class Skills; Shield Ally; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally; Merge Forms" e="A blood summoner conjures an eidolon born from violence and blood."}

::archetype[Broodmaster]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Life Link; Life Bond; Merge Forms" e="Most summoners bond with one eidolon for their entire lives, but the broodmaster forges a link with multiple smaller eidolons that make up his brood."}

::archetype[Counter-Summoner]{c=summoner r="Summon Monster; Bond Senses; Aspect; Greater Aspect" e="While most summoners excel at conjuring monsters, there are those who focus instead on disrupting the conjurations of opponents."}

::archetype[Evolutionist]{c=summoner r="Maker's Call; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally" e="An evolutionist possesses greater power over his eidolon's form, and is able to evolve his eidolon and its abilities to meet any challenge or face any threat as it comes up, seemingly on a whim."}
::archetype[First Worlder]{c=summoner r="Summon Monster; Eidolon" e="A first worlder summoner has a more primal connection to nature, their power being tied to the First World."}

::archetype[God Caller]{c=summoner r="Class Skills; Life Link; Transposition; Aspect; Greater Aspect; Twin Eidolon" e="God callers follow a fractured tradition that originated in the now-lost nation of Sarkoris. ::archetype[Naturalist]{c=summoner r="Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Aspect; Life Bond; Greater Aspect" e="A naturalist is a summoner who is in tune with the natural world, using his magic like a lens to focus various animal aspects onto his eidolon."}
::archetype[Pyroclast]{c=summoner r="Spellcasting; Eidolon; Maker's Call; Transposition" e="Pyroclasts harness the primal elemental energy of magma. Their eidolons often resemble elemental beings."}

::archetype[Shadow Caller]{c=summoner r="Class Skills; Summon Monster; Eidolon" e="(Fetchling Only) While most summoners can call any manner of creature from across the planes to serve them in combat, supplementing the skills of their eidolon with a diverse range of creatures, others eschew this broad utility and instead concentrate upon calling forth entities from the Shadow Plane."}

::archetype[Shaitan Binder]{c=summoner r="Share Spells; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Aspect; Greater Aspect; Twin Eidolon" e="(Oread Only) Shaitan binders call upon a reflection of their genie ancestors to serve as their eidolons."}

::archetype[Spirit Summoner]{c=summoner r="Summon Monster; Eidolon; Aspect; Maker's Call; Merge Forms; Transposition" e="A spirit summoner is an arcane spellcaster whose eidolon is a manifestation of a shamanic spirit."}

::archetype[Storm Caller]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition" e="Many summoners beckon monsters from the Elemental Planes, but few are as specialized as storm callers, who channel the elemental energies of the Plane of Air."}

::archetype[Story Summoner]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Bond Senses; Transposition" e="Story summoners rely on the harrow to weave the paths they walk, turning their tales into reality. They channel magic through their harrow decks to influence the strange extraplanar beings they summon, bringing familiar and fateful characters to life."}

::archetype[Synthesist]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Life Link; Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally; Merge Forms" e="Rather than summon an eidolon to serve by his side, the synthesist fuses his eidolon's essence to his own."}

::archetype[Twinned Summoner]{c=summoner r="Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Merge Forms" e="Reflecting the connected nature of Imbrex, a twinned summoner and his eidolon appear identical save for any obvious differences, such as evolutions or equipment."}

::archetype[Unwavering Conduit]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Summon Monster; Spells" e="Unwavering conduits work to keep everything ordered and regimented, drawing on power from the Eternal City of Axis, Heaven, or Hell to enforce whichever laws the summoner finds paramount."}

::archetype[Wild Caller (ARG)]{c=summoner r="Summon Monster; Eidolon" e="(Half-Elf Only) The wild caller calls eidolons that take more natural and savage forms and summons from nature rather than the Great Beyond."}

::archetype[Wild Caller (HotW)]{c=summoner r="Eidolon; Summon Monster; Aspect; Greater Aspect" e="A wild caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with an eidolon with a plant body."}

unchained_summoner.name: Unchained Summoner
description: ## Unchained Summoner
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Unchained/25›

There are those who take a different path when pursuing the arcane arts, reaching across the boundaries of the world to the far-flung planes to call forth all manner of creatures to do their bidding. Known as summoners, these arcane practitioners form close bonds with particular outsiders, known as eidolons, which increase in power along with their callers. In the end, summoners and their eidolons become linked, sharing shards of the same souls.

**Unchained:** Most of the summoner's class features are the same as those of the class's original design - the biggest change comes to the eidolon. The unchained summoner selects a subtype for his eidolon (such as ‹eidolon/angel›, ‹eidolon/demon›, or ‹eidolon/protean›), which determines a number of its evolutions and helps to shape the eidolon's general attitude and appearance. In addition, some of the evolutions are now tied to one or more subtypes and base forms, to make eidolons that better match the expected appearances and abilities of such creatures. Finally, the summoner spell list has been greatly revised, removing a number of imbalances.
**Alignment:** Any.
**Hit Die:** d8.
**Starting Wealth:** 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp).
::h3[Class Skills]{jl}

The Unchained Summoner's class skills are ‹skill/Craft› (Int), ‹skill/Fly› (Dex), ‹skill/Handle Animal› (Cha), ‹skill/Knowledge (all)› (Int), ‹skill/Linguistics› (Int), ‹skill/Profession› (Wis), ‹skill/Ride› (Dex), ‹skill/Spellcraft› (Int), and ‹skill/Use Magic Device› (Cha).
**Skill Points at each Level:** 2 + Int modifier.
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| 1st | +0 | +0 | +0 | +2 | Cantrips, eidolon, life link, *summon monster I* | 1 | - | - | - | - | - |
### Spells Known
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |

**Weapon and Armor Proficiency:** Summoners are proficient with all simple weapons and light armor. A summoner can cast summoner spells while wearing light armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance. Like any other arcane spellcaster, a summoner wearing medium or heavy armor, or using a shield, incurs a chance of arcane spell failure if the spell in question has a somatic component. A summoner still incurs the normal arcane spell failure chance for arcane spells granted by other classes.
::spellAb{caster="unchained summoner" learn m type=arcane stat=Charisma spontaneous limitedFull trade53 tradeLimit}
::cantrips

::ab[Eidolon (Basics)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power ability="You begin play with the ability to summon to your side a powerful outsider called an eidolon. The ‹sidekick/unchained_>eidolon› forms a link with you, who, forever after, summons an aspect of the same creature. Each eidolon has a ‹ability/unchained_eidolon_>subtype<s›, chosen when the eidolon is first summoned, that determines its origin and many of its abilities. Your eidolon must be within one alignment step of you (so a neutral good summoner can call a neutral, lawful good, or chaotic good eidolon) and can speak all of your languages. An eidolon is treated as a ‹spelldef/summon«ed creature<ing›, except that it is not sent back to its home plane until reduced to a number of negative hit points equal to or greater than its Constitution score. In addition, due to its tie to its summoner, an eidolon can touch and attack creatures warded by ‹spell/protection from evil› and similar effects that prevent contact with summoned creatures."}

A summoner can summon his eidolon in a ritual that takes 1 minute to perform. When summoned in this way, the eidolon's hit points are unchanged from the last time it was summoned. The only exception to this is if the eidolon was slain, in which case it returns with half its normal hit points. The eidolon does not heal naturally. The eidolon remains until dismissed by the summoner (a standard action). If the eidolon is sent back to its home plane due to death, it cannot be summoned again until the following day. The eidolon cannot be sent back to its home plane by means of ‹spell/dispel magic›, but spells such as ‹spell/dismissal› and ‹spell/banishment› function normally. If the summoner is unconscious, asleep, or killed, his eidolon is immediately banished.

The eidolon takes a form shaped by the summoner's desires. The eidolon's Hit Dice, saving throws, skills, feats, and abilities are tied to the summoner's class level and increase as the summoner gains levels. In addition, each eidolon gains a pool of evolution points based on the summoner's class level that can be used to give the eidolon different abilities and powers, called ‹ability/unchained_>evolutions›. Whenever the summoner gains a level, he must decide how these points are spent, and they are set until he gains another level of summoner.

The eidolon's physical appearance is up to the summoner, but it always appears as some sort of fantastical creature appropriate to its subtype. This control is not fine enough to make the eidolon appear like a specific creature. The eidolon also bears a glowing rune that is identical to a rune that appears on the summoner's forehead as long as the eidolon is summoned. While this rune can be hidden through mundane means, it cannot be concealed through magic that changes appearance, such as ‹spell/alter self› or ‹spell/polymorph› (although ‹spell/invisibility› does conceal it as long as the spell lasts).

::ab[Life Link (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=protect order=fp free="Whenever your eidolon takes enough damage to send it back to its home plane, as a reaction to the damage, you can sacrifice any number of hit points without using an action. Each hit point sacrificed in this way prevents 1 point of damage done to your eidolon. This can prevent your eidolon from being sent back to its home plane." passive="In addition, you and your eidolon must remain within 100 feet of one another for your eidolon to remain at full strength. If your eidolon is beyond 100 feet but closer than 1,000 feet, its current and maximum hit point totals are reduced by 50%. If your eidolon is more than 1,000 feet away but closer than 10,000 feet, its current and maximum hit point totals are reduced by 75%. If your eidolon is more than 10,000 feet away, it is immediately returned to its home plane. Current hit points lost in this way are not restored when your eidolon gets closer to its summoner, but its maximum hit point total does return to normal."}

::ab[Summon Monster I (Sp)]{jl="Summon Monster" l=1 icon=magic useMod3=Cha standard="You can cast ‹spell/summon monster I› as a spell-like ability. Drawing upon this ability uses up the same power as you use to call your eidolon. As a result, you can only use this ability when your eidolon is not summoned. When you cast this spell, the creatures remain for 1 minute per level (instead of 1 round per level)." special="You cannot have more than one *summon monster* or *gate* spell active in this way at one time. If this ability is used again, any existing *summon monster* or *gate* immediately ends. These summon spells are considered to be part of your spell list for the purposes of spell trigger and spell completion items. In addition, you can expend uses of this ability to fufill the construction requirements of any magic item you create, so long as you can use this ability to cast the required spell." imp3="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster II›." imp5="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster III›." imp7="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster IV›." imp9="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster V›." imp11="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VI›." imp13="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VII›." imp15="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster VIII›." imp17="This now behaves as ‹spell/summon monster IX›." imp19="This now behaves as either *summon monster IX* or ‹spell/gate›. If used as *gate,* you must pay any required material components."}

::ab[Bond Senses (Su)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=boost useL=summoner standard="You can share the senses of your eidolon, hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, and touching everything your eidolon does. There is no range to this effect, but your eidolon and you must be on the same plane. You can end this effect as a ***free action.***" hStandard}

::ab[Shield Ally (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=def passive="Whenever you are within your eidolon's reach, you gain a +2 shield bonus to your Armor Class and a +2 circumstance bonus on your saving throws. This bonus does not apply if your eidolon is ‹rule/grappled›, ‹misc/helpless›, ‹rule/paralyzed›, ‹misc/stunned›, or ‹misc/unconscious›."}

::ab[Maker's Call (Su)]{jl=strip l=6 icon=magic standard="You can call your eidolon to your side. This functions as ‹spell/dimension door›, using your caster level. When used, your eidolon appears adjacent to you (or as close as possible if all adjacent spaces are occupied). If your eidolon is out of range, the ability is wasted." useInc="4~summoner~6"}

::ab[Transposition (Su)]{jl=strip l=8 icon=magic standard="You can use your *maker's call* ability to swap locations with your eidolon. If it is larger than you, you can appear in any square occupied by your eidolon. The eidolon must occupy the square that was occupied by you if able, or as close as possible if it is not able."}

::ab[Aspect (Su)]{jl=strip l=10 icon=boost ability="You can divert up to 2 points from your eidolon's evolution pool to add evolutions to yourself. You cannot select any evolution that your eidolon could not possess, and you must be able to meet the requirements as well. You cannot select the *ability increase* evolution through this ability. Any points spent in this way are taken from your eidolon's evolution pool (reducing the total number available to your eidolon). You can change the evolutions you receive from these points any time you can change your eidolon's evolutions."}

::ab[Greater Shield Ally (Su)]{jl=strip l=12 icon=protect passive="Whenever an ally is within your eidolon's reach, the ally receives a +2 shield bonus to its Armor Class and a +2 circumstance bonus on its saving throws. If this ally is you, these bonuses increase to +4. This bonus does not apply if your eidolon is grappled, helpless, paralyzed, stunned, or unconscious."}

::ab[Life Bond (Su)]{jl=strip l=14 icon=def passive="As long as your eidolon has 1 or more hit points, you are protected from harm. Damage in excess of that which would reduce you to fewer than 0 hit points is instead transferred to your eidolon. Effects that cause death but not damage are unaffected by this ability. This ability does not affect spells like ‹spell/baleful polymorph›, ‹spell/flesh to stone›, or ‹spell/imprisonment›, or other spells that do not cause actual damage."}

::ab[Merge Forms (Su)]{jl=strip l=16 icon=def-power order=urw useL=summoner fullround="You can touch your eidolon and merge forms with it. This transformation includes all of your gear. While merged in this way, you are protected from harm and cannot be the target of spells or effects. All effects and spells currently targeting you are suspended until you emerge from your eidolon (although durations continue to expire)." special="You can cast spells while inside your eidolon by taking control of your eidolon for the duration of the casting. Any material components used for these spells are taken from your gear, even though they are otherwise inaccessible. You can direct all of your eidolon's actions while merged, can perceive through its senses, and can speak through its voice. If your eidolon is returned to its home plane while you are merged with it, you are immediately ejected, taking 4d6 points of damage, and are stunned for 1 round." swift="You can end this effect at any time. You emerge in a square adjacent to your eidolon if able."}

In addition, your eidolon loses 1 point from its evolution pool for every 2 points (or fraction thereof) diverted to you instead of losing 1 point from the evolution pool for each point diverted."}

::ab[Twin Eidolon (Su)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=power standard="You can assume the shape of your eidolon, copying all of its evolutions, form, and abilities. Your Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores change to match the base scores of your eidolon. You can choose to have any gear that you carry become absorbed by your new form, as with spells from the polymorph subschool. Items with continuous effects continue to function while absorbed in this way. You lose your natural attacks and all racial traits (except bonus feats, skills, and languages) in favor of the abilities granted by your eidolon's evolutions. You retain all of your class features. You can end this effect as a ***free action.***" hStandard useL=summoner useUnit=minute useNC="1-minute"}

::archetype[Soulbound Summoner]{c=unchained_summoner r="Eidolon; Life Link; Summon Monster" e="Soulbound summoners, as a rule, never set out to bind their soul to a dangerous and enigmatic power. Rather, they have found themselves unintentional masters of eidolons through incredible circumstances."}

::archetype[Storm Caller]{c=unchained_summoner r="Eidolon; Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition" e="Many summoners beckon monsters from the Elemental Planes, but few are as specialized as storm callers, who channel the elemental energies of the Plane of Air."}

::archetype[Twinned Summoner]{c=unchained_summoner r="Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Merge Forms" e="Reflecting the connected nature of Imbrex, a twinned summoner and his eidolon appear identical save for any obvious differences, such as evolutions or equipment."}

mesmerist.name: Mesmerist
description: ## Mesmerist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/38›

Experts at charm and deceit, mesmerists compel others to heed their words and bend to their will. Psychic powers, primarily those of enchantment and illusion, give mesmerists the tools they need to manipulate others - usually for their own personal gain. The very gaze of a mesmerist can hypnotize someone into following his whims. Mesmerists frequently form cults of personality around themselves, and they develop skills and contingency plans in case their ploys are discovered. They draw their magic from the Astral Plane, and many consider their minds to be conduits to enigmatic spaces others can't comprehend.

**Role:** Mesmerists wield power over lesser minds, suppressing foes' wills to weaken them. Priding themselves on their trickery and inventiveness, they also support their allies - and often themselves - with magical tricks, most of which offer protection. Their limited healing ability primarily provides ‹rule/temporary hit points›, so mesmerists aren't the strongest primary healers, but they can easily remove conditions that typically affect the mind.
::class{any hd=8 wealth=3 skp=6 app bluff craft diplo dis ea intm ka kd kh kl kno kr ling per perf prof sm soh spc stl umd}

| 2nd | +1 | +0 | +3 | +3 | Mesmerist trick, towering ego | 2 | - | - | - | - | - |

::prof{simple weaps="hand crossbow~sap~sword cane~whip" light}
::spellAb{type=psychic caster=mesmerist spontaneous stat=Charisma limitedFull trade53 tradeLimit m}
::cantrips{c=knacks}

::ab[Consummate Liar]{jl=strip icon=boost passive="You add 1/2 your mesmerist level (minimum 1) as a bonus on all Bluff checks. In addition, you qualify for the ‹feat/Improved Feint› and ‹feat/Greater Feint› feats, even if you don't have ‹feat/Combat Expertise› or an Intelligence score of at least 13. You can also ignore Combat Expertise and an Intelligence score of 13 as prerequisites for other feats that require Improved Feint or Greater Feint."}

::ab[Hypnotic Stare (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=lower swift="You can focus your stare on one creature within 30 feet. That creature takes a -2 penalty on Will saving throws. You can maintain your *stare* against only one opponent at a time; it remains in effect until you stare at a new target, the opponent dies, the opponent moves farther than 30 feet away, or you fall unconscious or die. You can remove the memory of your *stare* from the target's mind; the creature doesn't remember that it was affected (nor does it realize that it is currently being affected) unless you allow it." special="The *hypnotic stare* is a psychic effect, and relies more on your focus than the target's perception of your *stare.* It can't be avoided in the same ways a ‹umr/gaze› attack can. You can use this ability even while blinded, but must succeed at a DC 20 ‹rule/concentration› check to do so. Staring at a creature requires your focus, so if you use a gaze attack or similar ability, you must target the subject of your *hypnotic stare* or voluntarily end the *stare.* The penalties from multiple mesmerists' stares don't stack, nor do they stack with penalties from witches' ‹hex/evil eye› hexes. This is a mind-affecting effect." imp8="The penalty becomes -3."}

::ab[Mesmerist Tricks (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power-magic flavor="A mesmerist can create hypnotic bonds with his allies, implanting magical suggestions in their minds that you can later activate." usage="Half your mesmerist level (minimum 1) + your Charisma bonus (if any) tricks/day." hStandard standard="To implant a trick, you must either touch a willing creature or implant the trick in yourself. A creature can be the subject of only one *mesmerist trick* at a time. You can activate the trick as a ***free action*** when a triggering condition is met (as defined in the trick's description), even if it isn't your turn. The subject must be within medium range (100 feet + 10 feet per level) for you to trigger the trick. You monitor for the trick's triggering condition through a subtle telepathic connection, so you don't need line of sight to trigger it - but anything that blocks telepathic contact prevent you from triggering tricks. An implanted trick lasts until the next time you regain your spells. Each daily use of *mesmerist tricks* can be used to implant any trick you know." special="You can have only one trick implanted at a given time, and implanting a new trick ends the previous one (you still lose the use of this ability you spent on the previous trick). Once triggered, a trick is no longer implanted, and can't be triggered again until you implant the trick again. The duration of the effect caused by triggering a trick is either instantaneous or appears in the trick's entry. The DC for any *mesmerist trick* or *masterful trick* that requires a saving throw or skill check is 10 + 1/2 your mesmerist level + your Charisma modifier" passive="You know one trick at 1st level, and learn another trick at 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter (to a maximum of 11 tricks at 20th level). Here is the full list of ‹ability/mesmerist »tricks›. Unless specifically noted in the trick's description, you can't choose a particular trick more than once." order=usp}

::ab[Painful Stare (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=zap free="When an attack that deals damage hits the target of your *hypnotic stare,* you can cause the target to take an amount of additional damage equal to 1/2 your class level (minimum 1). You can use this ability even if it isn't your turn. If you use this ability to increase your own damage, the additional damage increases by 1d6 points for every 3 class levels you possess. This damage is ‹misc/precision damage› and is not multiplied on a critical hit. You can trigger this ability only once per round, but a single creature can take damage from multiple mesmerists' *painful stares* in a round."}

::ab[Towering Ego (Su)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="You gain a bonus equal to your Charisma bonus (minimum 0) on Will saving throws." special="If you are under any effect that would prevent you from providing the emotional component of psychic spells, you lose this bonus on saving throws."}

::ab[Bold Stare (Su)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=3 ability="Your *hypnotic stare* imposes a further effect upon its target, chosen from this list of ‹ability/mesmerist »stares›. The choice can't be changed later." special="You can't choose the same *bold stare improvement* more than once unless otherwise noted. All of your *bold stare improvements* affect the target as long as it is affected by your *hypnotic stare.*" imp7="You choose a new *bold stare improvement.*" imp11="You choose a new *bold stare improvement.*" imp15="You choose a new *bold stare improvement.*" imp19="You choose a new *bold stare improvement.*"}
:::aside
A mesmerist may take the ‹feat/Manifold Stare› feat in place of a bold stare improvement. The mesmerist must meet the feat's prerequisites.

::ab[Touch Treatment (Su)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=aid useMod3=Cha order=usw hStandard hImp standard="You can help allies shake off harmful conditions - especially those that affect their minds - by channeling psychic energy through your healing hands. You must be able to touch your target. You can remove one condition on the ***minor conditions*** list from your target each time you use this ability." swift="You can use *touch treatment* on yourself." imp6="You can remove one condition from the ***minor*** or ***moderate condition*** lists." imp10="You can remove one condition from the ***minor, moderate*** or ***greater condition*** lists." imp14="You can instead expend one use of touch treatment to replicate the effects of ‹spell/break enchantment›. This targets only one creature you are touching and follows all other restrictions of touch treatment."}
>@HL[Minor Conditions:] Fascinated, shaken.
>@HL[Moderate Conditions:] Confused, dazed, frightened, sickened.
>@HL[Greater Conditions:] Cowering, nauseated, panicked, stunned.

::ab[Manifold Tricks (Su)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost passive="You can have two *mesmerist tricks* implanted at a time." imp9="You can have three *mesmerist tricks* implanted at a time." imp13="You can have four *mesmerist tricks* implanted at a time." imp17="You can have five *mesmerist tricks* implanted at a time." special="Each creature can still be the subject of only one trick at a time. If you implant a new trick while you already have your maximum number of concurrent tricks implanted, you must choose one implanted trick to dismiss without triggering (the dismissed trick still expends a daily use of your tricks)."}

::ab[Mental Potency (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost-magic passive="You can affect more powerful creatures or a greater number of creatures than normal with your mental effects. Both the HD limit and the total number of HD affected with each enchantment or illusion spell you cast increase by 1. For enchantment and illusion spells you cast that target a number of creatures greater than one, the number of creatures affected also increases by one (so a spell that targets one creature per level would be affected, but a spell that targets only one creature would not be). For example, a 5th-level mesmerist could affect 5 HD worth of creatures with ‹spell/sleep›, affect 2d4+1 HD worth of creatures with ‹spell/hypnotism›, and change the categories for ‹spell/color spray› to &quot&3 HD or fewer,&quot& &quot&4 or 5 HD,&quot& and &quot&6 or more HD.&quot&" imp10="The HD limits now increase by 2." imp15="The HD limits now increase by 3." imp20="The HD limits now increase by 4."}

::ab[Glib Lie (Su)]{jl=strip l=11 icon=boost passive="You can deceive truth-detecting magic. A creature using this sort of magic against you must succeed at a caster level check against a DC of 15 + your class level to succeed (as if you were under the effect of a ‹spell/glibness› spell); failure means the magic doesn't detect your lies or force you to speak only the truth. This ability does not give you the *glibness* spell's bonus on Bluff checks."}

::ab[Masterful Tricks (Su)]{jl=strip l=12 icon=boost passive="You can choose a ‹ability/masterful trick<s› in place of a *mesmerist trick.* Using a *masterful trick* consumes two uses of the *mesmerist trick* class feature instead of one."}

::ab[Rule Minds (Su)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=power-magic ability="You can permanently mentally enslave a creature. When you successfully affect the target of your *hypnotic stare* with an enchantment spell that requires a saving throw, you can force that creature to attempt a second Will saving throw with a DC equal to the spell's DC. The DC is reduced by 5 if the monster isn't a humanoid. If the creature fails, it becomes a permanent thrall to you (as ‹spell/dominate monster›) until you die. If it succeeds, it can't be affected by *rule minds* again for 24 hours. You can have only one creature enthralled at a time. If you enthrall a new creature, the previous one is set free but is unaware that it was enthralled by you."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc
::altCapstone[Piercing Gaze (Su)]{deepMagics capstone="At 20th level, the mesmerist's gaze carries with it something of the beyond. His stare penalty increases by 2."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Dwarf:** Increase painful stare's damage by 1/4 point.[^A]
**Elf:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on Acrobatics checks to move through a threatened area and a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to feint.[^A]
**Elf:** Add a +1/4 bonus on grapple and trip combat maneuver checks.[^B]
**Gathlain:** Increase the number of mesmerist tricks the mesmerist can use per day by one-third.[^C]

**Ghoran:** Choose one mind-affecting mesmerist trick that the mesmerist knows. The mesmerist can affect intelligent plant creatures with that trick or spell as if they weren't immune to mind-affecting effects.[^D]
**Gnome:** Increase the number of mesmerist tricks the mesmerist can use per day by 1/3.[^E]
**Half-elf:** When casting mesmerist enchantment spells, add 1/2 to the effective caster level of the spell, but only to determine the spell's duration.[^E]
**Halfling:** Increase the number of mesmerist tricks the mesmerist can use per day by 1/3.[^E]
**Half-orc:** Increase the mesmerist's bonus damage from painful stare by 1/2 point.[^E]
**Human:** Increase the mesmerist's towering ego bonus by 1/3 point (to a maximum increase of +2).[^E]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^F]
**Kitsune:** Increase the number of mesmerist tricks the mesmerist can use per day by 1/3.[^F]
**Merfolk:** The mesmerist learns 1/4 of a new trick.[^G]

**Nagaji:** Add 1/5 to the Hit-Die limit and the total number of Hit Die affected with each enchantment or illusion spell the mesmerist casts. This bonus stacks with the mesmerist's mental potency class feature and is applicable under the same conditions as that ability.[^H]
**Ratfolk:** Gain a +1 bonus on concentration checks to maintain the mesmerist's hypnotic stare.[^I]
**Tengu:** Add 1/4 to the AC bonus provided by the meek facade mesmerist trick.[^J]
**Wyvaran:** Add 1/4 to the mesmerist's level for the purpose of determining the effect of mesmerist tricks.[^K]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Aromaphile]{c=mesmerist r="Hypnotic Stare; Painful Stare; Towering Ego; Bold Stare; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency" e="(Ghoran only) Rather than employ the baleful glares of the typical mesmerist, aromaphiles hypnotize others using an array of sickeningly sweet scents developed from their unique ghoran physiology."}
::archetype[Autohypnotist]{c=mesmerist r="Hypnotic Stare; Mental Potency" e="An autohypnotist has incredibly powerful psychic abilities that he can't entirely control."}

::archetype[Chart Caster]{c=mesmerist r="Class Skills; Mesmerist Tricks; Manifold Tricks; Touch Treatment" e="Lirgeni astrologists would divine their futures by consulting star charts, but some had no talent for astrology and simply faked their results. When prophecy died with Aroden, a few Lirgeni survivors continued with this &quot&reliable&quot& method."}

::archetype[Cult Master]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; Painful Stare; Mesmerist Tricks; 3rd, 6th, and 14th-level Touch Treatments; 7th-level Bold Stare; Masterful Tricks; Rule Minds" e="The leaders of some cults are simply charlatans, bathing in the adulation of their followers. Others believe themselves to be truly in touch with an unknown power, bringing their unique messages to those willing to follow and obey them."}

::archetype[Dreamstalker]{c=mesmerist r="Painful Stare; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Towering Ego; Touch Treatment; Manifold Tricks; Glib Lie" e="Many mesmerists concentrate their studies on conscious, rational thought, but dreamstalkers instead delve into the unleashed unconscious mind that wanders free in dreams."}

::archetype[Enigma]{c=mesmerist r="Hypnotic Stare; Consummate Liar; Painful Stare; Touch Treatment; Manifold Tricks; Glib Lie; Rule Minds" e="An enigma spends his life dedicated to developing psychic abilities that allow him to operate unnoticed."}

::archetype[Eyebiter]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency; Glib Lie" e="An eyebiter's eyes become so infused with psychic might that they can leave the eyebiter's body and move about on their own."}

::archetype[Fey Trickster]{c=mesmerist r="Class Skills; Spellcasting; Towering Ego; Touch Treatment; Rule Minds" e="Most mesmerists draw psychic power from the Astral Plane, but a few gain their powers in an unusual way: from a brush with the fey."}
::archetype[Gaslighter]{c=mesmerist r="Alignment; Consummate Liar; Touch Treatment; Painful Stare" e="A gaslighter takes joy in others' suffering, delighting in driving them to madness."}

::archetype[Hate-Monger]{c=mesmerist r="Alignment; Consummate Liar; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency; Manifold Tricks; 7th-level Bold Stare; Glib Lie" e="Hate-mongers are xenophobic and judgmental rabblerousers who whip up mobs against enemies real or imagined."}

::archetype[Material Manipulator]{c=mesmerist r="Spellcasting; Consummate Liar; 2nd-level Mesmerist Trick; Manifold Tricks; Touch Treatment; Rule Minds" e="Rather than waste time limiting himself to ruling minds, a material manipulator forces his whims upon reality and uses his magic to ensure that those around him comply with his demands."}

::archetype[Mindwyrm Mesmer]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; Painful Stare" e="Mindwyrm mesmers emulate the confidence, swagger, and fear inspired by dragons to bully and browbeat others into obedience."}

::archetype[Projectionist]{c=mesmerist r="Spellcasting; 4th-level Mesmerist Trick; Mental Potency" e="Not content to simply command others from afar, a projectionist is able to use his mesmerizing powers to utterly dominate other creatures and objects by projecting his spirit from his body."}

::archetype[Spirit Walker]{c=mesmerist r="Spells; Consummate Liar; Mental Potency; 3rd, 6th, 10th, and 14th-level Touch Treatments; Rule Minds" e="Some mesmerists specialize in dominating and controlling undead rather than the living."}

::archetype[Thought Eater]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; Towering Ego; Touch Treatment; Rule Minds" e="Mesmerists that learn to pierce deep into the minds of their victims are known as thought eaters, dangerous individuals capable of devouring thoughts and assuming the knowledge those notions represent for themselves."}

::archetype[Toxitician]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; Hypnotic Stare; Painful Stare; Bold Stare; Touch Treatment; Glib Lie" e="Toxiticians forgo the mesmerist's stare, instead combining their psychic power with their alchemical knowledge to craft injections that torment their foes and bolster themselves and their allies."}

::archetype[Umbral Mesmerist]{c=mesmerist r="Spellcasting; 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th-level Mesmerist Tricks; Towering Ego; Mental Potency" e="Trained in secret, umbral mesmerists are clandestine weapons who spend years learning how to shape terrifying creatures from the inky darkness in which they revel."}

::archetype[Vexing Daredevil]{c=mesmerist r="Class Skills; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Bold Stare; Glib Lie" e="Vexing daredevils train their psychic powers for combat, specializing in momentarily blinding and confusing their foes with sudden feints and tricks."}
::archetype[Vexing Trickster]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar, Touch Treatment" e="Vexing tricksters indulge in the various hijinks their power enables."}

::archetype[Vizier]{c=mesmerist r="Consummate Liar; 3rd-level Bold Stare; Towering Ego; Mental Potency" e="While many mesmerists focus on beguiling their foes or taking overt control of others with their mental powers, viziers carve out a subtler niche for themselves as advisors."}

::archetype[Vox]{c=mesmerist r="Spellcasting; Towering Ego; 10th-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Hypnotic Stare" e="Most mesmerists practice their mental manipulation through their entrancing gaze, but others have learned to practice their skill through the lilt and intonation of their voice."}
