rogue.name: Rogue
description: ## Rogue
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/67›

Life is an endless adventure for those who live by their wits. Ever just one step ahead of danger, rogues bank on their cunning, skill, and charm to bend fate to their favor. Never knowing what to expect, they prepare for everything, becoming masters of a wide variety of skills, training themselves to be adept manipulators, agile acrobats, shadowy stalkers, or masters of any of dozens of other professions or talents. Thieves and gamblers, fast talkers and diplomats, bandits and bounty hunters, and explorers and investigators all might be considered rogues, as well as countless other professions that rely upon wits, prowess, or luck. Although many rogues favor cities and the innumerable opportunities of civilization, some embrace lives on the road, journeying far, meeting exotic people, and facing fantastic danger in pursuit of equally fantastic riches. In the end, any who desire to shape their fates and live life on their own terms might come to be called rogues.

**Role:** Rogues excel at moving about unseen and catching foes unaware, and tend to avoid head-to-head combat. Their varied skills and abilities allow them to be highly versatile, with great variations in expertise existing between different rogues. Most, however, excel in overcoming hindrances of all types, from unlocking doors and disarming traps to outwitting magical hazards and conning dull-witted opponents.
::class{any hd=8 wealth=4 skp=8 acro app bluff climb craft diplo ddev dis ea intm kd kl ling per perf prof sm soh stl swim umd}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +0 | +0 | +2 | +0 | Sneak attack +1d6, trapfinding |
| 2nd | +1 | +0 | +3 | +0 | Evasion, rogue talent |
| 3rd | +2 | +1 | +3 | +1 | Sneak attack +2d6, trap sense +1 |
| 4th | +3 | +1 | +4 | +1 | Rogue talent, uncanny dodge |
| 5th | +3 | +1 | +4 | +1 | Sneak attack +3d6 |
| 6th | +4 | +2 | +5 | +2 | Rogue talent, trap sense +2 |
| 7th | +5 | +2 | +5 | +2 | Sneak attack +4d6 |
| 8th | +6/+1 | +2 | +6 | +2 | Improved uncanny dodge, rogue talent |
| 9th | +6/+1 | +3 | +6 | +3 | Sneak attack +5d6, trap sense +3 |
| 10th | +7/+2 | +3 | +7 | +3 | Advanced talents, rogue talent |
| 12th | +9/+4 | +4 | +8 | +4 | Rogue talent, trap sense +4 |
| 15th | +11/+6/+1 | +5 | +9 | +5 | Sneak attack +8d6, trap sense +5 |
| 18th | +13/+8/+3 | +6 | +11 | +6 | Rogue talent, trap sense +6 |
| 20th | +15/+10/+5 | +6 | +12 | +6 | Master strike, rogue talent |
::prof{simple weaps="hand crossbow~rapier~sap~shortbow~short sword" light}

::ab[Sneak Attack]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost flavor="If a rogue can catch an opponent when he is unable to defend himself effectively from her attack, she can strike a vital spot for extra damage." ability="Your attack deals extra damage any time your target would be denied a Dexterity bonus to AC (whether the target actually has a Dexterity bonus or not), or when you ‹rule/flank<ed› your target. This extra damage is a number of d6s equal to 1 + half your rogue level. (1d6 at first, 2d6 at 3rd, 3d6 at 5th, and so on.) You must be able to see the target well enough to pick out a vital spot and must be able to reach such a spot. You cannot sneak attack while striking a creature with ‹rule/concealment›." special="If you score a critical hit with a sneak attack, this extra damage is not multiplied. Ranged attacks can count as sneak attacks only if the target is within 30 feet. With a weapon that deals nonlethal damage (like a sap, whip, or an unarmed strike), you can make a sneak attack that deals nonlethal damage instead of lethal damage. You cannot use a weapon that deals lethal damage to deal nonlethal damage in a sneak attack, not even with the usual -4 penalty."}

::ab[Trapfinding]{jl=strip l=1 icon=def-power passive="You add 1/2 your level to Perception skill checks made to locate traps and to Disable Device skill checks (minimum +1). You can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps."}

::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="If you make a successful Reflex saving throw against an attack that normally deals half damage on a successful save, you instead take no damage. Evasion can be used only if the you are not ‹misc/helpless› and are wearing light armor or no armor."}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{jl=strip flavor="As a rogue gains experience, she learns a number of talents that aid her and confound her foes." l=2 icon=power ability="You gain one ‹ability/rogue talent›. You gain an additional rogue talent at every even-numbered level (4th, 6th, 8th, and so on)." special="You cannot select an individual talent more than once. Some talents add effects to your *sneak attack.* Only one of these talents can be applied to an individual *attack* and the decision must be made before the attack roll is made."}

::ab[Trap Sense (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=def passive="You gain a bonus on Reflex saves made to avoid traps and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by traps. Both of these bonuses are equal to ‹misc/one-third› of your rogue level." special="Trap sense bonuses gained from multiple classes stack."}

::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=def passive="You cannot be caught ‹rule/flat-footed›, nor do you lose your Dex bonus to AC if the attacker is invisible. You still lose your Dexterity bonus to AC if immobilized, or if an opponent successfully uses the ‹rule/feint› action against you." special="If you already have uncanny dodge from a different class, you automatically gain improved uncanny dodge instead."}

::ab[Improved Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You can no longer be ‹rule/flanked› This defense denies another rogue the ability to *sneak attack* you by flanking you, unless the attacker has at least four more rogue levels than you do." special="If you already have uncanny dodge from another class, the levels from the classes that grant uncanny dodge stack to determine the minimum rogue level required to flank you."}
::ab[Advanced Talents]{jl="Other Abilities" icon=boost l=10 ability="You can choose an ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› in place of a *rogue talent.*"}

::ab[Master Strike (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-lower ability="Each time you deal *sneak attack* damage, you can choose one of the following three effects: the target can be put to sleep for 1d4 hours, ‹rule/paralyzed› for 2d6 rounds, or slain. Regardless of the effect chosen, the target receives a Fortitude save to negate the additional effect. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your rogue level + your Intelligence modifier." special="Once a creature has been the target of a master strike, regardless of whether or not the save is made, that creature is immune to that rogue's master strike for 24 hours. Creatures that are immune to *sneak attack* damage are also immune to this ability."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Masterful Talent (Ex)]{theRightSpot capstone="At 20th level, the rogue has been a thief, an actor, a merchant, a scout, a confessor, a friend, an assassin, and a dozen more things besides. The rogue gains a +4 bonus on all of her skills."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aphorite:** Add a +1 bonus on Disable Device and Use Magic Device checks related to glyphs, scrolls, symbols, and other magical writings.[^A]
**Catfolk:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to feint and Sleight of Hands checks to pickpocket.[^B]
**Changeling:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^C]
**Dhampir:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Stealth checks and Perception checks made in ‹rule/dim light› or darkness.[^D]
**Drow:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to feint and pass secret messages.[^E]
**Duskwalker:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^F]
**Dwarf:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Disable Device checks regarding stone traps and a +1/2 bonus to trap sense regarding stone traps.[^G] [^H]

**Elf:** Add +1 to the number of times per day the rogue can cast a cantrip or 1st-level spell gained from the minor magic or major magic talent. The number of times this bonus is selected for the major magic talent cannot exceed the number of times it is selected for the minor magic talent. The rogue must possess the associated rogue talent to select these options.[^I]
**Fetchling:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Stealth and Sleight of Hand checks made while in ‹rule/dim light› or darkness.[^J]
**Ganzi:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Stealth checks while on a plane other than the Material Plane.[^K]
**Gathlain:** Add a +1/2 bonus to Bluff checks to pass secret messages or create a distraction to hide and on Sleight of Hands checks to pickpocket.[^L] [^M]

**Ghoran:** Choose one type of ranger ‹ability/favored enemy› (or one subtype in case of either humanoid or outsider). The rogue adds 1/2 to her sneak attack damage when using sneak attack against creatures of the chosen type (maximum +3).[^N]
**Gillman:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^O]
**Gnome:** The rogue gains a +1/2 bonus on Disable Device and Use Magic Device checks related to glyphs, symbols, scrolls, and other magical writings.[^P] [^Q]
**Gnome:** Add 1/4 to the Perception and Disable Device DCs of traps the rogue creates.[^R]
**Goblin:** Add a +1 bonus on the rogue's sneak attack damage rolls during the surprise round or before the target has acted in combat.[^S] [^T]
**Grippli:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Perception checks while in a forest or swamp.[^U]
**Half-elf:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to feint and Diplomacy checks to gather information.[^V] [^W]

**Halfling:** Choose a weapon from the following list: sling, dagger, or any weapon with "halfling" in its name. Add a +1/2 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls with that weapon (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^X] [^Y]
**Half-orc:** Add +1/3 on critical hit confirmation rolls made while using sneak attack (maximum bonus of +5). This bonus does not stack with Critical Focus.[^Z]

**Hobgoblin:** Reduce the penalty for not being proficient with one weapon by 1. When the nonproficiency penalty for a weapon becomes 0 because of this ability, the rogue is treated as having the appropriate Martial or Exotic Weapon Proficiency feat for that weapon.[^1]
**Human:** The rogue gains +1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^2] [^3]
**Ifrit:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Acrobatics checks to jump and a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks to demoralize enemies.[^4]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^5]
**Kitsune:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^6]
**Kobold:** Add +1/2 to the rogue's trap sense bonus to AC.[^7]
**Locathah:** Choose a piercing melee weapon. Add a +1/2 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls with that weapon (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^8]
**Ratfolk:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Escape Artist checks.[^9]
**Samsaran:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^0]
**Sylph:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Acrobatics checks to jump and a +1/2 bonus on Sense Motive checks.[^!]

**Tengu:** Choose a weapon from those listed under the tengu's swordtrained ability. Add a +1/2 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls with that weapon (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with Critical Focus.[^@]
**Tiefling:** Add +1/2 to sneak attack damage dealt to creatures with the outsider type.[^&]
**Vanara:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^%]
**Vine leshy:** Add a +1/2 bonus to Climb checks and on Stealth checks in forests.[^#]
**Vishkanya:** Add +1 to the rogue's CMD when resisting a grapple or reposition attempt.[^;]
**Wayang:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a new rogue talent.[^?]
**Wyrwood:** The rogue gains 1/6 of a rogue talent.[^_]
**Wyvaran:** Add a +1/2 bonus to Stealth and Sleight of Hand checks to steal or hide objects.[^=]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}
::archetype[Acrobat]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="Whether they are daring thieves, infiltrating assassins, or intrepid spies, proper training in acrobatics is a valuable boon for rogues."}

::archetype[Bandit]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge" e="Bandits tend to have a variety of skills; sometimes these skills are similar to a ranger's, but a bandit's tactics always deal with surprise followed by intimidation, with the clear threat of naked force if intimidation does not do the trick."}

::archetype[Bekyar Kidnapper]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="A Bekyar kidnapper is not only adept at hiding herself in thick jungles and quickly subduing her prey, but also at spreading rumors of hidden ruins, buried treasure, and other such valuables that might lure an unwary foreigner into her traps."}

::archetype[Bellflower Irrigator]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 4th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Rather than focusing on saving slaves, Bellflower irrigators seek to defeat slavery by aiming to eliminate the source."}
::archetype[Burglar]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Adept at infiltration, trap removal, and lock picking, burglars prey on the homes of the wealthy and forgotten tombs alike."}

::archetype[Carnivalist]{c=rogue r="2nd, 4th, 6th-level Rogue Talents; Sneak Attack; Trap Sense" e="Carnivalists train their miniature minions to perform acts of larceny and often hide their true talents behind theatrical sideshows."}

::archetype[Cat Burglar]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="(Catfolk Only) Cat burglars are masters of breaking and entering, using their feline grace to make it seem as though no crime was ever committed in the first place."}
::archetype[Chameleon]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="An absolute master of disguise, a chameleon effortlessly blends into any environment."}
::archetype[Charlatan]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="A charlatan is a master of lies and deception."}

::archetype[Consigliere]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trapfinding; Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; 10th-level Rogue Talent" e="Consiglieres act as trusted mediators and administrators for crime families and various criminal organizations, and many serve as the right hand to the leaders of such groups."}

::archetype[Construct Saboteur]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense" e="The construct saboteur harnesses arcane energy to combat the golems and eldritch guardians that prowl the depths of ancient ruins and forgotten tombs."}

::archetype[Counterfeit Mage]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 4th-level Rogue Talent" e="Charlatans and stage magicians use sleight of hand to fake magic. A counterfeit mage goes a step further, parroting the motions and activation phrases used by arcane casters to activate wands or other magical accoutrements."}

::archetype[Cutpurse]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="Cutpurses are often trained by guilds to collect the guild's tax from local businesses on a daily basis, whether through intimidation or pickpocketing. Some find themselves taking up the mantle of adventurer, and their talents are generally appreciated in this role, but the cutpurse is still the first person her companions look to when an item goes missing."}
::archetype[Dark Lurker]{c=rogue r="Evasion; 2nd, 8th, 14th, and 20th-level Rogue Talents" e="Dark lurkers use darkness as an ally."}

::archetype[Deadly Courtesan]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="(Vishkanya Only) Skilled at manipulation and diversion, the deadly courtesan builds up those around her and periodically takes them down."}
::archetype[Desert Raider]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="Desert raider learn the secrets of the deserts and become inured to their dangers."}

::archetype[Discretion Specialist]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense/Danger Sense; 4th, 12th-level Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Discretion specialists deal with bodies, inconvenient witnesses, and other loose ends."}

::archetype[Dreamthief]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Sneak Attack; Master Strike; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 12th-level Rogue Talent" e="Each dreamthief bears a focused fragment of emotion, known as a dreamshard, within her soul. This metaphysical crystal grants a dreamthief supernatural powers, which she supplements with skill and grace."}
::archetype[Driver]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="A driver makes her living driving vehicles in the service of those who can pay for her considerable talents."}

::archetype[Earthshadow]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Evasion; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Rogue Talents" e="In the centuries since the Quest for the Sky, the mystic lore the dwarves call earthcraft has been passed down through the generations, and its practitioners are called earthshadows."}

::archetype[Eldritch Raider]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Skill Ranks; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense" e="(Gillman Only) An eldritch raider is a rogue who seeks to unravel the mysteries of the destruction of the gillmen's homeland."}

::archetype[Eldritch Scoundrel]{c=rogue r="Armor Proficiency; Skills; Trap Sense; Sneak Attack; Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Students of arcane magic, legerdemain, and stealth, eldritch scoundrels are a rare breed of adventurer most commonly found seeking lost and valuable arcane writings in the ruins of fallen empires, such as Thassilon or the Jistka Imperium."}

::archetype[Escapologist]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="The escapologist excels at escaping any confinement, whether physical, magical, or mental. Few restraints can hold an escapologist for long."}

::archetype[False Medium]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense" e="The false medium is a master of pretending to have occult powers in order to manipulate the emotions and atmosphere of the people and places around him, often reaping a healthy reward from the bereaved, who find solace in his comforting lies."}

::archetype[Fey Prankster]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd, 6th, 8th, and 12th-level Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="(Gathlain) Fey pranksters specialize in tricking others to teach a lesson or for their own entertainment."}

::archetype[Filcher]{c=rogue r="Evasion; Trap Sense 1-6; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="(Halfling Only) Whether cutting purses in the midst of combat or replacing prized items with fakes under the noses of their owners, the filcher is the master of the quick and quiet steal."}

::archetype[Galtan Agitator]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Rogue Talent" e="Galtan agitators fight mightily to cast down the enemies of their ideals within the shambles of the tumultuous nation of Galt."}

::archetype[Guerrilla]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Evasion; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Guerrillas fight against oppressive leadership and governments including Chelish colonization in Sargava and the power structures in Cheliax proper, Nidal, and Razmiran"}

::archetype[Guild Agent]{c=rogue r="Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Evasion; Master Strike" e="Guild agents are members of a thieves' guild or other shadowy organization. What they lose in independence, they more than make up for in benefits gained due to their association's infrastructure."}

::archetype[Gun Smuggler]{c=rogue r="Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Sneak Attack; Trap Finding; 6th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Gun smugglers are adept at procuring firearms and concealing their secret weapons"}

::archetype[Heister]{c=rogue r="2nd, 4th, 8th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Notorious for their stealth, imagination, and elaborately planned thefts and capers, heisters specialize in breaking into secure locations, stealing items of value, and escaping undetected."}

::archetype[Investigator]{c=rogue r=Trapfinding e="Not all rogues work against the law. Investigators use their skills to serve the law, often in the employ of nobles or in the pursuit of noble causes."}

::archetype[Kintargo Rebel]{c=rogue r="Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge" e="Whenever the Chelish navy sails into the port, these rebels are quick to appear agreeably subservient in order to conceal their non-compliance and avoid retribution."}
::archetype[Kitsune Trickster]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="(Kitsune Only) The kitsune trickster combines her sharpened wit with minor arcane powers of charm and persuasion."}
::archetype[Knife Master]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Sneak Attack; Trap Sense" e="The knife master is a trained killer who specializes in close-up combat and the wave and weave of knife fighting."}

::archetype[Makeshift Scrapper]{c=rogue r="Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 12th-level Rogue Talent" e="Some combatants specialize in the study of certain weapons, but those without formal training often learn to survive a fight by making brilliant use of whatever is lying around."}

::archetype[Master of Disguise]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd, 10th-level Rogue Talent" e="A master of disguise believes that never letting her adversaries know her true identity protects her from bad luck or missteps on a job."}

::archetype[Nameless Shadow]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 14th, 18th-level Rogue Talents" e="Where bandits and tyrants prey on merchants and caravans, the nameless shadow preys on bandits and tyrants."}

::archetype[Needler]{c=rogue r="Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; 6th-level Rogue Talent; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Needlers use subtlety and misdirection to deliver deadly poisons to unsuspecting victims in creative and unorthodox ways."}

::archetype[Numerian Scavenger]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="A Numerian scavenger lives for the discovery of new technological wonders scraped from the depths of the strange ruins, metal caverns, and outlandish junkyards of Numeria."}

::archetype[Okeno Liberator]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge" e="The sight of yellow sails on the Inner Sea signals the approach of a ship from Okeno, home port of the most ruthless and feared of all slaving operations. Most who see these sails flee in terror, but some freedom fighters actually endeavor to be captured, knowing that being taken to Stonespine Island in manacles is the easiest way to infiltrate the city, liberate other slaves, and lead them to freedom."}

::archetype[Phantom Thief]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Sneak Attack; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Master Strike" e="Phantom thieves come from the ranks of the elite, having grown bored with their finery, and seek thrills from acts of daring, skill, and crime."}
::archetype[Pirate]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trapfinding" e="A pirate breaks from the confines of country and king to commit her crimes upon the high seas."}

::archetype[Planar Sneak]{c=rogue r="Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="A planar sneak finds creative ways to defy conventional limitations when trying to overcome extraplanar obstacles."}
::archetype[Poisoner]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="The poisoner knows poison is just a tool toward an end, and is no different than any other weapon."}
::archetype[Rake]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="The rake is a rogue who is open about her skills and talents, often to the point of being boastful."}

::archetype[Relic Raider]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Relic raiders are rogues who specialize in recovering treasures from ruins and tombs, some in a quest for knowledge, others out of a desire for riches."}

::archetype[River Rat]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="River rats are skilled at hiding amid reeds and roots, striking the unwary from the shallows as others would from the shadows."}
::archetype[Roof Runner]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="A specialized urban acrobat, the roof runner makes her home high atop the spires and gables of great cities."}

::archetype[Rotdrinker]{c=rogue r="Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Poisons are common tools in a rogue's trade, and some poison-using rogues are so frequently exposed to these toxins that they have developed resistances to particular poisons."}

::archetype[Sanctified Rogue]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="The sanctified rogue serves a higher purpose, acting as a representative of a church or cult, or following the tenets of a specific faith or deity."}
::archetype[Sapper]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd and 4th-level Rogue Talents" e="The sapper is a trained siege engineer and can deal with traps and other obstacles given plenty of time."}

::archetype[Scout]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Scouts frequently roam the wilderness, often banding together as bandits, but sometimes serving as guides, as trailblazers, or as companions to a ranger or barbarian warrior."}

::archetype[Scroll Scoundrel]{c=rogue r="2nd-level Rogue Talent, Trap Sense, Uncanny Dodge, Improved Uncanny Dodge, 10th-level Rogue Talent" e="Using knowledge from extensive research, the scroll scoundrel exploits overconfidence and predictability to better stay alive."}

::archetype[Sczarni Swindler]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Evasion; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="If life is a game, the Sczarni play to win. When skill and luck aren't enough, cheating is often the best option."}

::archetype[Seeker of the Lost]{c=rogue r="2nd-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Aquatic elven adventurers descending into the ruins of sunken Azlant developed techniques to ease their explorations, and they now share these techniques among a loose group of acquaintances calling themselves the seekers of the lost."}

::archetype[Shadow Scion]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd, 8th, 14th-level Rogue Talent; Master Strike" e="Shadow scions have learned the secrets of light and dark and draw power from the shadows themselves."}

::archetype[Shadow Walker]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Shadow walkers are comfortable in light, darkness, and the shadows in between."}
::archetype[Sharper]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 16th-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense" e="Sharpers use trickery and deception to part an owner from his valuables."}

::archetype[Skulking Slayer]{c=rogue r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Trapfinding; Rogue Talent; Advanced Rogue Talent; Trap Sense 1-4" e="(Half-Orc Only) Pushed into a life of crime by the society around them, half-orcs gravitate toward criminal activities that suit them best."}
::archetype[Sly Saboteur]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Sly saboteurs are irregular soldiers who specialize in sabotage and surprise attacks."}
::archetype[Smuggler]{c=rogue r="Trap Sense; Trapfinding; Evasion" e="Smugglers specialize in moving contraband from one area to another while avoiding detection by authorities."}

::archetype[Snare Setter]{c=rogue r="Sneak Attack; Trapfinding; Master Strike" e="(Kobold Only) Deep in darkness, the snare setter constructs his deadly traps and brutal mechanisms to confound and destroy any who try to invade his domain."}

::archetype[Sniper]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="Some say that the sniper is the worst kind of assassin: a killer who waits silently in the shadows and then strikes from a distance without remorse."}

::archetype[Snoop]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 8th-level Rogue Talent" e="Snoops act as information peddlers, specializing in gathering secrets through investigation, subterfuge, and coercion, and selling or trading those secrets for personal gain."}

::archetype[Spy]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="The best spies are like chameleons, but not only can they change their appearances to fit the situation, they can also change their personalities, allegiances, and even loves if that's what it takes to achieve their clandestine goals."}
::archetype[Survivalist]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="The survivalist focuses her talents on surviving harsh and unforgiving conditions that would kill a lesser rogue."}

::archetype[Swamp Poisoner]{c=rogue r="Racial Traits; Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense" e="(Grippli) Swamp poisoners use their own toxins to defend their homes and level the playing field"}

::archetype[Swashbuckler]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="A paragon of mobile swordplay, the swashbuckler is a rogue who focuses almost exclusively on honing her skill at arms and perfecting daring acrobatic moves and elaborate flourishes that border on performance."}

::archetype[Swordmaster]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trap Sense" e="(Tengu Only) A swordmaster meditates to strengthen her spiritual connection to her blade. She strives to perfect her skills by mastering six deadly trances."}

::archetype[Sylvan Trickster]{c=rogue r="Class Skills; Trapfinding; Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Sylvan tricksters are rogues who model themselves after the mischievous fey of legend."}

::archetype[Thug]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense" e="Some criminals steal with finesse, their victims only discovering the crime when the rogue is long gone and the coin already spent. A thug, on the other hand, cares nothing for finesse."}

::archetype[Tidal Trickster]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 4th, 8th-level Rogue Talent" e="Tidal tricksters roam the seas, using their skillful mastery of the currents to move across aquatic battlefields and throw their enemies off-balance."}

::archetype[Toxic Talon]{c=rogue r="Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="A toxic talon spends resources and time combining easy-to-obtain materials into dangerous toxins to aid in the defense of Andoran."}

::archetype[Trapsmith]{c=rogue r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Some rogues are not content with just disabling traps - they love to build them, finding a captivating beauty in the turning of gears and the slither of ropes over pulleys."}
::archetype[Underground Chemist]{c=rogue r="Evasion; 4th-level Rogue Talent; Advanced Talents" e="Underground chemists are part of the rotting, fetid underbelly of the alchemical world."}

::archetype[Vexing Dodger]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Vexing dodgers take advantage of their smaller size to outmaneuver larger foes such as giants."}

::archetype[Waylayer]{c=rogue r="Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 12th and 20th-level Rogue Talents" e="A clever rogue knows the easiest battle to win is the one that's over before your foe knows it has begun."}

unchained_rogue.name: Unchained Rogue
sources: The Elephant in the Room: Feat Taxes in Pathfinder
description: ## Unchained Rogue
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Unchained/20›

Thief, sneak, charmer, diplomat - all of these and more describe the rogue. When not skulking in the shadows, disarming traps, and stealing from the unaware, rogues may rub shoulders with powerful nobles or plot capers with fellow crooks. The rogue is the master of many faces, using her skills and talents to get herself both into and out of trouble with rakish aplomb. While others may call them charlatans and burglars, even the most larcenous rogues tend to consider themselves consummate professionals, willing to take on any job if the price is right.

**Unchained:** While much of the unchained rogue will be familiar to those who have played the class from the *Core Rulebook,* there are a number of new class features that greatly enhance the power and flexibility of the rogue. Chief among these is the debilitating injury class feature. A rogue with this ability can severely hamper her foes, giving her a much-needed boost to her offense or defense, depending on the situation. In addition, with finesse training, the rogue now gains ‹feat/Weapon Finesse› for free at 1st level. This ability also lets her add her Dexterity to damage rolls with one weapon starting at 3rd level. Finally, the rogue's edge ability ties into a new system called skill unlocks. With this feature, the rogue can master a small set of chosen skills, outperforming all those characters without access to such talents.

‹SOURCE The Elephant in the Room: Feat Taxes in Pathfinder›

At 1st level, a rogue gains @hll[rule/Deft Maneuvers] as a bonus feat. In addition, starting at 3rd level, she can select any one ‹ability/weapon group<s› listed under the fighter's Weapon Training class feature. Once this choice is made, it cannot be changed. Whenever she makes a successful melee attack with a ‹rule/finesse› weapon or an ‹magic-enh/agile› weapon from this group, she adds her Dexterity modifier instead of her Strength modifier to the damage roll. If any effect would prevent the rogue from adding her Strength modifier to the damage roll, she does not add her Dexterity modifier. The rogue can select a second weapon group at 11th level and a third at 19th level.
